---
title: Inventory
execute:
  warning: false
---

An [`Inventory`](`dascore.core.inventory.Inventory`) stores richer metadata than a [`Patch`](`dascore.core.patch.Patch`) normally carries. It can describe acquisitions, instruments, instrument configurations, optical paths, fiber geometry, coupling conditions, and annotations.

Inventory metadata is not automatically applied to patches. The patch remains the primary data object, and inventory context is added explicitly when you want it.

The common workflow is:

1. Use an acquisition id to resolve the relevant inventory context.
2. Add selected acquisition/instrument metadata to patch attrs.
3. Add selected optical-path metadata to patch coordinates.
4. For channel-indexed patches, first derive optical distance from the inventory.

:::{.callout-note}
Inventory support is append-only and time-aware. If multiple records with the same resource id exist, `Inventory.view(...)` uses the patch time to choose the effective record.
:::

# Load an example inventory

DASCore includes example inventories which describe some example patches.

```{python}
import dascore as dc
from dascore.examples import get_example_inventory

patch = dc.get_example_patch("random_das")
inventory = get_example_inventory("random_das")

print(inventory)
```

An inventory usually contains many record types. An [`Acquisition`](`dascore.core.inventory.Acquisition`) is the record that links patch-like data to an instrument configuration and an optical path.

```{python}
acquisition = inventory.view("random_das_acquisition")

print(acquisition.instrument_configuration)
print(acquisition.optical_path)
```

Patch methods use `patch.attrs.acquisition_id` by default, so set it when the patch does not already have one.

```{python}
patch = patch.update_attrs(acquisition_id="random_das_acquisition")
```

# Add attrs from inventory

Use [`Patch.add_inventory_attrs`](`dascore.proc.inventory.add_inventory_attrs`) to copy selected acquisition, instrument configuration, or instrument fields into patch attrs.

```{python}
patch_with_attrs = patch.add_inventory_attrs(
    inventory,
    attrs=("tag", "sample_rate", "gauge_length", "instrument.model"),
)

print(patch_with_attrs.attrs.tag)
print(patch_with_attrs.attrs.sample_rate)
print(patch_with_attrs.attrs.gauge_length)
print(patch_with_attrs.attrs.instrument_model)
```

Simple attr names are accepted only when they are unambiguous across the acquisition graph. Qualified names use one of these prefixes:

- `acquisition.`
- `instrument_configuration.`
- `instrument.`

For example, `instrument.model` is stored on the output patch as `instrument_model`.

# Add coordinates from inventory

Use [`Patch.add_inventory_coords`](`dascore.proc.inventory.add_inventory_coords`) to project optical-path metadata onto the patch's `distance` coordinate.

By default, only `label` is added.

```{python}
patch_with_label = patch.add_inventory_coords(inventory)

print(patch_with_label.get_array("label")[:5])
```

Geometry axes are explicit. This is important because one inventory may use local `x, y, z` axes while another may use `latitude, longitude, elevation`.

```{python}
patch_with_xyz = patch.add_inventory_coords(
    inventory,
    coords=("label", "x", "y", "z"),
)

print(patch_with_xyz.get_array("x")[:5])
print(patch_with_xyz.get_array("z")[:5])
```

If an axis is requested but unavailable, DASCore raises by default. Use `on_missing="nan"` when missing geometry axes should be represented by `NaN`.

```{python}
patch_with_missing_geo = patch.add_inventory_coords(
    inventory,
    coords=("latitude", "longitude", "elevation"),
    on_missing="nan",
)

print(patch_with_missing_geo.get_array("latitude")[:5])
```

:::{.callout-important}
There is intentionally no `"geometry"` wildcard shortcut in the first implementation. Requesting explicit coordinate names makes downstream code predictable: either the patch has the requested coordinates, or the method raises.
:::

# Build geometry from a table

If you have surveyed locations along a cable, use [`Inventory.from_geometry_table`](`dascore.core.inventory.Inventory.from_geometry_table`) to create a geometry-only inventory from control points. The table needs a `distance` column in meters. Other columns become coordinate axes.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
from dascore.core import inventory as inv

geometry = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "distance": [0.0, 100.0, 250.0],
        "x": [0.0, 0.0, 50.0],
        "y": [0.0, 10.0, 30.0],
        "z": [0.0, -100.0, -180.0],
    }
)

survey_inventory = inv.Inventory.from_geometry_table(
    geometry,
    id_prefix="survey_1",
)

print(survey_inventory.view("survey_1:optical_path"))
```

This builder only creates CRS, geometry, and optical-path records. Add an acquisition record to link the path to patch data.

```{python}
survey_inventory = survey_inventory.add(
    inv.Acquisition(
        resource_id="survey_1_acquisition",
        optical_path_id="survey_1:optical_path",
    )
)

survey_patch = patch.update_attrs(acquisition_id="survey_1_acquisition")
survey_patch = survey_patch.select(distance=(0, 250))
survey_patch = survey_patch.add_inventory_coords(
    survey_inventory,
    coords=("x", "y", "z"),
)

print(survey_patch.get_array("x")[:5])
```

# Project interval metadata

Inventory coordinate projection can also attach metadata from optical path interval records. Simple field names work when they are unambiguous.

```{python}
patch_with_fiber = patch.add_inventory_coords(
    inventory,
    coords=("label", "fiber_type", "coupling_type"),
)

print(patch_with_fiber.get_array("fiber_type")[:5])
print(patch_with_fiber.get_array("coupling_type")[:5])
```

Qualified interval metadata can be requested with one of these prefixes:

- `optical_component.`
- `geometry.`
- `coupling_condition.`

Qualified names are stored on the patch using the requested coordinate name.

```{python}
patch_with_meta = patch.add_inventory_coords(
    inventory,
    coords=("geometry.geometry_type", "coupling_condition.quality"),
)

print(patch_with_meta.get_array("geometry.geometry_type")[:5])
print(patch_with_meta.get_array("coupling_condition.quality")[:5])
```

# Channel-indexed data

Some formats store an integer channel or index dimension rather than optical distance. In that case, first derive `distance` from the acquisition's instrument configuration with [`Patch.distance_from_inventory`](`dascore.proc.inventory.distance_from_inventory`).

```{python}
import numpy as np

channel_patch = dc.get_example_patch("random_das")
n_channels = channel_patch.channel_count
channel_patch = channel_patch.rename_coords(distance="channel")
channel_patch = channel_patch.update_coords(
    channel=np.arange(n_channels),
)
channel_patch = channel_patch.update_attrs(acquisition_id="random_das_acquisition")

distance_patch = channel_patch.distance_from_inventory(inventory, dim="channel")

print(distance_patch.get_array("distance")[:5])
```

Once `distance` exists, use the same coordinate projection methods as before.

```{python}
enriched = distance_patch.add_inventory_coords(
    inventory,
    coords=("label", "x", "fiber_type"),
)

print(enriched.get_array("label")[:5])
print(enriched.get_array("x")[:5])
print(enriched.get_array("fiber_type")[:5])
```

# Using a different example inventory

The `example_event_2` inventory describes a borehole-style optical path. It uses the same inventory methods, but the geometry and annotations come from a different acquisition context.

```{python}
event_patch = dc.get_example_patch("example_event_2")
event_inventory = get_example_inventory("example_event_2")
event_patch = event_patch.update_attrs(acquisition_id="example_event_2_acquisition")

event_patch = event_patch.add_inventory_attrs(
    event_inventory,
    attrs=("tag", "data_units", "sample_rate"),
)
event_patch = event_patch.add_inventory_coords(
    event_inventory,
    coords=("label", "z", "coupling_type"),
)

print(event_patch.attrs.tag)
print(event_patch.get_array("label")[:5])
print(event_patch.get_array("z")[:5])
print(event_patch.get_array("coupling_type")[:5])
```

# Missing values

For attrs, missing fields or missing linked records raise by default. Use `on_missing="ignore"` to skip unavailable attrs.

```{python}
patch_ignore = patch.add_inventory_attrs(
    inventory,
    attrs=("tag", "instrument.serial_number"),
    on_missing="ignore",
)

print(patch_ignore.attrs.tag)
```

For geometry coordinates, use `on_missing="nan"` to add requested axes even when the inventory cannot provide them.

```{python}
patch_nan = patch.add_inventory_coords(
    inventory,
    coords=("latitude",),
    on_missing="nan",
)

print(patch_nan.get_array("latitude")[:5])
```
